title: KEEP IN OREGON - 211info
text: KEEP KEEP IN OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers online weekly peer support groups for resource parents, adoptive/guardian and kinship families caring for children/youth in their home. Groups are led by trained group leaders using evidence based parenting strategies and trauma informed curriculum. Groups take place for 16 weekly, 90 minute sessions. Each participating household receives $25 for each session and up to 24 ODHS training credits. 10 Shelton McMurphey Boulevard Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Resource/kinship families with a child age 3 and older in the home. Kinship families caring for relative children/youth and not involved with ODHS may be eligible for the KEEP for KIN Program. Hours: Not applicable (541) 485-2711 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 485-2711 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Adoption and Foster/Kinship Care Support Groups In Person Support Groups * Foster Parents Other Services Offered At This Location: MUTUAL SUPPORT - KEEP CONNECTING KIN PARENTS If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. KEEP
